name: ID

aliases.name: ID
aliases.comment:
The basis for making a ID type, has one generic type argument which should be an internal type to the module the ID type is defined in.

    module UserID exposing(ID)

    import ID

    type alias ID = ID.ID IDInternal

    type IDInternal = IDInternal

values.name: debugID
values.comment: Create an ID with an arbitrary value for debugging purposes. Requires 'Debug.todo' as a first argument
values.type: (String.String -> Basics.Never) -> Basics.Int -> ID.ID a

values.name: decoder
values.type: Json.Decode.Decoder (ID.ID a)

values.name: encode
values.type: ID.ID a -> Json.Encode.Value

values.name: toString
values.comment: Converts an ID into a string representing the number inside the ID
values.type: ID.ID a -> String.String

name: ID.Counter

unions.name: Counter
unions.comment: Counter type which is used to create [`ID`](ID#ID) values

values.name: accountForDictIDs
values.comment: Same as [`accountForIDs`](ID.Counter#accountForIDs) but can be used with a [`Dict`](ID.Dict#Dict)
values.type: ID.Dict.Dict (Internal.ID a) value -> ID.Counter.Counter (Internal.ID a) -> ID.Counter.Counter (Internal.ID a)

values.name: accountForIDs
values.comment:
Account for a list of [`ID`](ID#ID)s.
This should not be necessary if you always make sure to use the counter result from [`newID`](ID.Counter#newID), but can be used to sanitize the counter after encoding and decoding a counter and values
values.type: List.List (Internal.ID a) -> ID.Counter.Counter (Internal.ID a) -> ID.Counter.Counter (Internal.ID a)

values.name: accountForItemIDs
values.comment:
Same as [`accountForIDs`](ID.Counter#accountForIDs) but can be used for a list of values containing an [`ID`](ID#ID)

    newCounter = accountForItemIDs .id users counter
values.type: (item -> Internal.ID a) -> List.List item -> ID.Counter.Counter (Internal.ID a) -> ID.Counter.Counter (Internal.ID a)

values.name: decoder
values.type: Json.Decode.Decoder (ID.Counter.Counter id)

values.name: encode
values.type: ID.Counter.Counter id -> Json.Encode.Value

values.name: giveIDs
values.comment: Asign [`ID`](ID#ID)s to a number of values, make sure to use the new counter to create any new IDs
values.type: (Internal.ID a -> b -> c) -> ID.Counter.Counter (Internal.ID a) -> List.List b -> ( List.List c, ID.Counter.Counter (Internal.ID a) )

values.name: new
values.type: a -> ID.Counter.Counter (Internal.ID a)

values.name: newID
values.comment: Creates a new [`ID`](ID#ID) and advanced the counter, make sure to use the new counter to create any new IDs
values.type: ID.Counter.Counter (Internal.ID a) -> ( Internal.ID a, ID.Counter.Counter (Internal.ID a) )

values.name: newID2
values.comment: Same as [`newID`](ID.Counter#newID) but creates 2 [`ID`](ID#ID)s instead of 1
values.type: ID.Counter.Counter (Internal.ID a) -> ( Internal.ID a, Internal.ID a, ID.Counter.Counter (Internal.ID a) )

values.name: newID3
values.comment: Same as [`newID`](ID.Counter#newID) but creates 3 [`ID`](ID#ID)s instead of 1
values.type: ID.Counter.Counter (Internal.ID a) -> ( { id1 : Internal.ID a, id2 : Internal.ID a, id3 : Internal.ID a }, ID.Counter.Counter (Internal.ID a) )

values.name: newID4
values.comment: Same as [`newID`](ID.Counter#newID) but creates 4 [`ID`](ID#ID)s instead of 1
values.type: ID.Counter.Counter (Internal.ID a) -> ( { id1 : Internal.ID a, id2 : Internal.ID a, id3 : Internal.ID a, id4 : Internal.ID a }, ID.Counter.Counter (Internal.ID a) )

values.name: sanitizeIDs
values.comment: Account for all used [`ID`](ID#ID)s in the counter (as in [`accountForIDs`](ID.Counter#accountForIDs)) and give new [`ID`](ID#ID)s to any items that have a duplicate [`ID`](ID#ID)
values.type: (val -> Internal.ID a) -> (val -> Internal.ID a -> val) -> List.List val -> ID.Counter.Counter (Internal.ID a) -> ( List.List val, ID.Counter.Counter (Internal.ID a) )

name: ID.Dict

values.name: decoder
values.type: Json.Decode.Decoder value -> Json.Decode.Decoder (ID.Dict.Dict id value)

values.name: diff
values.type: ID.Dict.Dict (Internal.ID a) b -> ID.Dict.Dict (Internal.ID a) c -> ID.Dict.Dict (Internal.ID a) b

values.name: empty
values.type: ID.Dict.Dict (Internal.ID a) value

values.name: encode
values.comment: Encode a `Dict` to a JSON value
values.type: (value -> Json.Encode.Value) -> ID.Dict.Dict id value -> Json.Encode.Value

values.name: filterMap
values.type: (Internal.ID a -> b -> Maybe.Maybe c) -> ID.Dict.Dict (Internal.ID a) b -> ID.Dict.Dict (Internal.ID a) c

values.name: fold
values.type: (Internal.ID a -> value -> c -> c) -> c -> ID.Dict.Dict (Internal.ID a) value -> c

values.name: fromList
values.type: List.List ( Internal.ID a, value ) -> ID.Dict.Dict (Internal.ID a) value

values.name: get
values.type: Internal.ID a -> ID.Dict.Dict (Internal.ID a) value -> Maybe.Maybe value

values.name: getFrom
values.comment: Same as [`get`](ID.Dict#get) but with the arguments swapped, useful for pipelines
values.type: ID.Dict.Dict (Internal.ID a) value -> Internal.ID a -> Maybe.Maybe value

values.name: ids
values.type: ID.Dict.Dict (Internal.ID a) value -> List.List (Internal.ID a)

values.name: insert
values.type: Internal.ID a -> value -> ID.Dict.Dict (Internal.ID a) value -> ID.Dict.Dict (Internal.ID a) value

values.name: intersect
values.comment: Same as [`Dict.intersect`](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/elm/core/latest/Dict#intersect), but can accept dictionaries with different value types
values.type: ID.Dict.Dict (Internal.ID a) b -> ID.Dict.Dict (Internal.ID a) c -> ID.Dict.Dict (Internal.ID a) b

values.name: isEmpty
values.type: ID.Dict.Dict (Internal.ID a) value -> Basics.Bool

values.name: map
values.type: (Internal.ID a -> b -> c) -> ID.Dict.Dict (Internal.ID a) b -> ID.Dict.Dict (Internal.ID a) c

values.name: member
values.type: Internal.ID a -> ID.Dict.Dict (Internal.ID a) value -> Basics.Bool

values.name: partition
values.type: (Internal.ID a -> value -> Basics.Bool) -> ID.Dict.Dict (Internal.ID a) value -> ( ID.Dict.Dict (Internal.ID a) value, ID.Dict.Dict (Internal.ID a) value )

values.name: remove
values.type: Internal.ID a -> ID.Dict.Dict (Internal.ID a) value -> ID.Dict.Dict (Internal.ID a) value

values.name: size
values.type: ID.Dict.Dict (Internal.ID a) value -> Basics.Int

values.name: toList
values.type: ID.Dict.Dict (Internal.ID a) value -> List.List ( Internal.ID a, value )

values.name: union
values.type: ID.Dict.Dict (Internal.ID a) value -> ID.Dict.Dict (Internal.ID a) value -> ID.Dict.Dict (Internal.ID a) value

values.name: update
values.comment: Same as [`Dict.update`](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/elm/core/latest/Dict#update) but does not operate on Maybe values
values.type: Internal.ID a -> (value -> value) -> ID.Dict.Dict (Internal.ID a) value -> ID.Dict.Dict (Internal.ID a) value

values.name: values
values.type: ID.Dict.Dict (Internal.ID a) value -> List.List value

name: ID.Set

aliases.name: Set
aliases.comment: A set type for IDs, is equal to a [`ID.Dict'](ID.Dict#Dict) with empty tuples as values

values.name: decoder
values.type: Json.Decode.Decoder (ID.Set.Set id)

values.name: diff
values.type: ID.Set.Set (Internal.ID a) -> ID.Set.Set (Internal.ID a) -> ID.Set.Set (Internal.ID a)

values.name: empty
values.type: ID.Set.Set (Internal.ID a)

values.name: encode
values.type: ID.Set.Set id -> Json.Encode.Value

values.name: fold
values.comment: Alias for [`ID.Dict.fold'](ID.Dict#fold) but without a value argument
values.type: (Internal.ID a -> c -> c) -> c -> ID.Set.Set (Internal.ID a) -> c

values.name: fromList
values.comment: Create a set from a list of IDs
values.type: List.List (Internal.ID a) -> ID.Set.Set (Internal.ID a)

values.name: insert
values.comment: Alias for [`ID.Dict.insert'](ID.Dict#insert) but without a value
values.type: Internal.ID a -> ID.Set.Set (Internal.ID a) -> ID.Set.Set (Internal.ID a)

values.name: intersect
values.type: ID.Set.Set (Internal.ID a) -> ID.Set.Set (Internal.ID a) -> ID.Set.Set (Internal.ID a)

values.name: isEmpty
values.type: ID.Set.Set (Internal.ID a) -> Basics.Bool

values.name: member
values.type: Internal.ID a -> ID.Set.Set (Internal.ID a) -> Basics.Bool

values.name: remove
values.type: Internal.ID a -> ID.Set.Set (Internal.ID a) -> ID.Set.Set (Internal.ID a)

values.name: size
values.type: ID.Set.Set (Internal.ID a) -> Basics.Int

values.name: toList
values.type: ID.Set.Set (Internal.ID a) -> List.List (Internal.ID a)

values.name: union
values.type: ID.Set.Set (Internal.ID a) -> ID.Set.Set (Internal.ID a) -> ID.Set.Set (Internal.ID a)
